sections.past: EUR/USD slipped 12 pips to 1.1756 as the dollar recovered modestly on higher Treasury yields. DXY bounced off multi-month lows near 98.20.
sections.why: Dollar weakness remains the dominant theme -- DXY is now lower than when the Iran conflict began -- but rising US 10Y yields at 4.41% are providing a floor for the greenback.
sections.watch: US CPI data this week is the key catalyst. A hot print could spark a dollar bounce toward 1.1650; a miss keeps the EUR bid toward 1.1850.

headlines.title: The Euro’s Next Move Will Make or Break FIDI’s 2026 Returns
Dollar Slides on Stock Strength and Weak Consumer Sentiment
Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes

sections.past: Cable fell 59 pips to 1.3536 in a broad dollar rebound session, retreating from recent highs. UK election concerns added headwinds.
sections.why: Sterling has been one of the best-performing G10 currencies in 2026, but upcoming UK elections are creating political uncertainty that threatens the pound's resilient run.
sections.watch: UK labor market data due this week. Strong wage growth keeps BoE on hold and supports GBP; any softening opens 1.3450.

headlines.title: Yen Spikes to 10-Week High and Sparks Intervention Chatter
UK Elections Threaten to End the Pound’s Resilient Run
Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes

sections.past: USD/JPY rallied 60 pips to 157.46 as the yen gave back its 10-week high spike. BoJ intervention chatter faded as the pair stabilized.
sections.why: The rate differential story is back -- US 10Y at 4.41% vs Japan's near-zero policy rate keeps carry trade demand alive, despite BoJ verbal warnings above 155.
sections.watch: Japan GDP data this week and any BoJ commentary. Break above 158 risks another intervention scare; a hold below 157 signals yen bears are losing steam.

sections.past: Aussie eased 17 pips to 0.7220, consolidating near multi-month highs. Commodity prices and China optimism continue to underpin.
sections.why: The Australian dollar rally has been driven by iron ore strength and improved China trade sentiment, but the Trump-Xi summit this week adds event risk.
sections.watch: Australian wage data and the Trump-Xi meeting are the dual catalysts. Positive trade signals could push AUD past 0.7300; breakdown below 0.7170 invalidates the rally.

headlines.title: The Dollar is Now Lower Than When the Iran War Began
Dollar softens as Middle East ceasefire holds
Why is the Australian Dollar Rallying?

sections.past: Gold pulled back $14.70 to $4,704 after touching near all-time highs last week. Profit-taking emerged as equities hit fresh records.
sections.why: Gold remains in a structural bull market driven by central bank buying and geopolitical hedging, but at $4,700+, positioning is stretched and vulnerable to any peace deal headlines.
sections.watch: US CPI this week determines the next leg -- hot inflation revives rate-cut doubts and could cap gold; soft data unlocks $4,800+. Iran headlines remain the wildcard.

sections.past: Silver dropped $0.58 to $84.90, underperforming gold slightly on the session. Industrial demand concerns tempered the bid.
sections.why: The gold-silver ratio has compressed as silver benefits from both precious metal demand and industrial usage in solar/EV sectors, but $85 is a key psychological barrier.
sections.watch: Gold's reaction to CPI will drive silver directionally. A break above $87 opens a run to $90; failure at $85 could see retracement to $80 support.

Oil climbs but markets shrug off US-Iran deadlock
K92 Mining Q1 Profit Jumps 66%, Revenue Hits Record on Higher Production and Prices

sections.past: WTI surged $2.42 to $100.49 after Trump rejected Iran's peace terms, prolonging the effective Strait of Hormuz closure and supply disruption fears.
sections.why: Oil is the direct trade for Mideast war risk -- the Strait of Hormuz handles 20% of global oil flow, and every failed negotiation attempt reprices supply disruption higher.
sections.watch: Any Iran-US diplomatic signal is the binary catalyst. A deal collapses crude back toward $90; continued stalemate or escalation targets $110. OPEC+ response also in focus.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow rises, S&P 500 and Nasdaq eke out records as chip stocks extend gains

Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Monday, May 11, 2026: Bitcoin's strongest opening in months

sections.past: S&P 500 eked out another record close at 7,413, up 0.19%, led by chip stocks extending their AI-driven rally. Breadth was narrow.
sections.why: Markets are treating geopolitics as background noise, focusing instead on blowout corporate earnings -- Mag 7 accounts for 55% of S&P earnings growth this quarter.
sections.watch: US CPI on Tuesday and Powell's last public comments as Fed Chair. A hot CPI could finally break this buy-the-dip reflex; futures are already sliding pre-market.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slide as Wall Street braces for CPI report

Massive AI investments come with one downside for Big Tech investors

sections.past: Nasdaq 100 gained 0.29% to 29,321, posting a fresh all-time high. Memory chip stocks hit records on AI demand; Lumentum cracked past $1,000.
sections.why: The AI capex cycle is accelerating -- $725B in planned AI spending from four megacap companies is replacing tens of thousands of tech workers, concentrating returns in infrastructure plays.
sections.watch: CPI data and any new chip export restrictions from the Trump-Xi summit could swing the index. Mega-cap tech earnings revisions remain the structural tailwind.

sections.past: Dow rose 95 points to 49,704, lagging the tech-heavy Nasdaq. Intel surged on its AI pivot under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, replacing Nvidia in the Dow conversation.
sections.why: The Dow's underperformance reflects its old-economy tilt in an AI-driven market. Value rotation needs a catalyst -- either a tech correction or rate cuts that favor cyclicals.
sections.watch: CPI and the Trump China trip are the week's pivots. Dow futures are already slipping pre-market on inflation anxiety. 50,000 remains the psychological magnet.

sections.past: Bitcoin pulled back $664 to $81,064, retreating from its strongest opening of the week. Crypto broadly softened alongside risk assets late in the session.
sections.why: BTC is stuck between institutional accumulation via ETFs and macro headwinds -- oil above $100 and rising Treasury yields create a risk-off undertow that caps upside at $85K.
sections.watch: CPI data this week is critical for rate-cut expectations. A dovish print could spark a breakout above $83K toward $85K; hot inflation risks a flush to $78K support.

sections.past: ETH fell $37 to $2,302, underperforming Bitcoin as the altcoin rotation narrative stalled. Solana developer share gains continued to pressure ETH sentiment.
sections.why: Ethereum faces a dual challenge -- developer migration to competing L1s and Tom Lee's BitMine buying $12B in ETH wasn't enough to shift momentum. The ETH/BTC ratio keeps grinding lower.
sections.watch: Ethereum needs to reclaim $2,400 to signal the correction is over. CPI-driven macro moves will dominate, but any Pectra upgrade timeline news could provide a catalyst.

sections.past: NVIDIA rallied $4.24 to $219.44, up nearly 2%, as memory chip stocks hit record highs on insatiable AI demand. Intel's AI pivot added sector tailwinds.
sections.why: NVIDIA remains the clearest AI capex beneficiary -- every major hyperscaler is expanding GPU orders, and the new Intel partnership validates NVIDIA's platform dominance beyond standalone chips.
sections.watch: Trump's China trip creates chip export risk. Any tightening of AI chip sales to China would dent the $725B capex narrative; any loosening is fuel for $230+.

headlines.title: Intel stock rises as CEO Lip-Bu Tan touts 'exciting new products' with Nvidia
Memory chip stocks hit record highs, pharma reacts to hantavirus concerns
Arbe Robotics (ARBE) Expands Beyond Automotive Into Defense and Robotics Markets

sections.past: Tesla surged 3.89% to $445, the session's top Mag 7 performer. Piper Sandler's note that buying here gets Optimus robotics exposure 'for free' drove buyer enthusiasm.
sections.why: The bull thesis has shifted from cars to Optimus and autonomous driving -- at current prices, the market is paying full price for the auto business and getting the robotics optionality as a bonus.
sections.watch: Musk is joining Trump's China trip. Any Tesla-specific trade or factory deals would be a catalyst. Cybertruck RWD recall is noise. $450 resistance is the near-term test.

headlines.title: Tesla: Buying at these levels means you get Optimus for 'free', Piper Sandler says
Qualcomm China Trip Puts AI And Chip Export Risks In Focus
Jeff Bezos-Backed Slate Auto To Take On Tesla: Is Billionaire Now Distancing Himself From Startup?

sections.past: Apple slipped $0.37 to $292.68, flat in a session where chip stocks stole the spotlight. Tim Cook confirmed he's joining Trump's China trip.
sections.why: Apple's China exposure is both its biggest opportunity and risk -- 20% of iPhone revenue comes from Greater China, and the Trump-Xi summit will directly impact tariff and supply chain dynamics.
sections.watch: The China trip outcome is the week's catalyst for AAPL. Any tariff relief or supply chain commitments could push it past $300; escalation drags it toward $285.

headlines.title: Tesla's Elon Musk, Apple's Tim Cook among CEOs set to join Trump on China trip
Memory chip stocks hit record highs, pharma reacts to hantavirus concerns
Qualcomm China Trip Puts AI And Chip Export Risks In Focus

sections.past: Microsoft fell $2.46 to $412.66 as CEO Satya Nadella testified in the Musk-OpenAI trial, revealing he was never told why Altman was fired. Shares drifted lower on distraction risk.
sections.why: The OpenAI trial exposes governance risks in Microsoft's biggest AI bet -- any adverse ruling could restructure the $13B investment. Meanwhile, the African data center stumble shows execution gaps.
sections.watch: Trial developments and CPI data are the dual catalysts. The AI capex narrative remains intact but legal overhang needs resolution. $420 resistance, $400 support.

Microsoft CEO says he was never given 'clarity' on why Altman was fired from OpenAI during Musk trial

sections.past: Meta dropped 1.77% to $598.86, the worst Mag 7 performer. Reports of an internal AI crisis and workforce backlash against Zuckerberg's AI strategy weighed on shares.
sections.why: Meta's $3.3B AI investment is drawing scrutiny as internal dissent grows and layoffs accelerate -- the market is asking whether the spend is strategic or defensive panic.
sections.watch: Any resolution of the internal AI leadership tensions could stabilize the stock. $580 is key support; a break below opens $560. CPI macro backdrop dominates short-term.

headlines.title: By the numbers: Corporate profits haven't been this smoking hot in years

Meta’s AI crisis deepens: Inside the fight against Mark Zuckerberg

sections.past: Alphabet dropped 3.03% to $388.64, the sharpest decline among megacaps. Anthropic's partnership news and ongoing AI competitive pressure triggered the sell-off.
sections.why: Search disruption fear is real -- Anthropic's SpaceX data center deal signals accelerating competition, and the market is repricing Google's AI moat as narrower than previously assumed.
sections.watch: Google I/O announcements and any Gemini model updates could provide a counter-narrative. $380 is the nearest support; failure there targets $370. AI competitive dynamics drive sentiment.

headlines.title: By the numbers: Corporate profits haven't been this smoking hot in years
Entergy Shareholders Back Board as CEO Highlights Data Center Demand, Grid Investments
Anthropic Just Delivered Great News for Alphabet Investors

sections.past: Amazon fell $3.69 to $268.99, pressured by the broad tech sell-off despite bullish AI moat commentary from analysts. Trucking subsidiary news added marginal support.
sections.why: Amazon's AI advantage is AWS Trainium and Inferentia chips eating into NVIDIA's inference market -- the quiet moat keeps expanding, but the stock needs a macro catalyst to decouple from the tech sell-off.
sections.watch: AWS growth metrics in the next earnings update and any China tariff news from Trump's trip. $265 support holds the uptrend; break below targets $255.

macro_context.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq post fresh records as markets shrug off US-Iran stalemate; oil surges past $100 after Trump rejects peace terms while CPI looms as the week's defining catalyst.
